name: Artichokes Alla Giudea
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the outer layers of deep green leaves from each artichoke and discard them.
2. Cut off the stem and set aside.
3. Rub each cut surface with a lemon wedge.
4. Place an artichoke on its side and cut off the top third, discarding these points.
5. Using scissors, carefully cut off the sharp tips of the remaining leaves.
6. Soak the artichokes for 10 minutes in cold water mixed with the juice of 1 lemon.
7. Gently open each leaf by prying them apart as if opening a flower and press down on the counter to separate the edges. Drain on paper towels, shaking off any excess liquid. Season between leaves and all over with salt and pepper.
8. Heat the virgin olive oil in a tall saucepan (8-inch to 10-inch wide) until it just begins to smoke.
9. Carefully place the artichokes in the hot oil and cook for 12 to 15 minutes, or until golden brown on all sides.
10. After 10 minutes of cooking, mist the artichokes with water by gently shaking your hand over them to prevent splattering.
11. Remove each artichoke from the pan using a fork and press the flower side down to open it further.
12. Drain on paper towels, season again with salt and pepper, and serve with lemon wedges.
